anchored: verify_data vs an independent crypto/hmac (oracle leg) — guards the vector
verify_data vs ComputeFinished (impl leg) + VerifyFinished accept/reject — guards the impl
not_covered_here: High/Sovereign (SHA-384, HashLen 48): this vector is Standard/SHA-256

name: N-PAMP Finished KAT

npamp_inputs.description: finished_key_* are fixed fixtures; th_scv/th_ccv are the Transcript KAT's points (transcript-kat.json) that the Finished MAC covers per spec §6.2.

provenance: Standards-derived, non-circular. verify_data = HMAC-SHA256(finished_key, transcript_hash). The expected verify_data are produced with crypto/hmac directly, NOT handshakedraft00.ComputeFinished — so they are independent of the implementation under test. The HMAC-SHA-256 primitive is anchored to RFC 4231 TC1/TC2 (the consuming test reproduces those published MACs before trusting verify_data). transcript_hash inputs are the Transcript KAT's real TH_sCV (server) / TH_cCV (client); finished_key values are fixed fixtures (the key-schedule KAT anchors finished_key DERIVATION separately).

rfc4231_hmac_sha256.description: RFC 4231 §4.2/§4.3 HMAC-SHA-256 test cases; anchor the HMAC-SHA-256 primitive.
